name: compound-v skill triggering
description: Test cases for compound-v skill auto-trigger discovery. Run with the Skill Creator's eval harness or your preferred prompt-test runner.

tests.name: positive-1: brainstorming output triggers compound-v
tests.prompt: I just finished superpowers:brainstorming. The spec is for adding Notion OAuth to our existing OAuth gateway. What's next?

expect.rationale: Description explicitly says 'Use when superpowers:brainstorming has produced a spec'. Direct trigger language.

tests.name: positive-2: writing-plans output triggers compound-v
tests.prompt: writing-plans just produced a plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-notion-oauth.md. Ready to execute.

expect.rationale: Description: 'OR when superpowers:writing-plans has produced a plan'. Plan path + execution intent = trigger.

tests.name: positive-3: about-to-execute triggers compound-v
tests.prompt: I'm about to use superpowers:subagent-driven-development to implement this plan in parallel.

expect.rationale: Description: 'OR when about to invoke superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans'.

tests.name: negative-1: greenfield feature without superpowers context
tests.prompt: I want to start a brand new React app from scratch. What's the best CLI to scaffold it?

expect.rationale: No mention of superpowers, brainstorming, plans, or execution phase. Compound V should not fire.

tests.name: negative-2: pure bash debugging
tests.prompt: My grep command isn't matching what I expect. Help me debug the regex.

expect.rationale: No multi-step implementation context, no plan, no spec. Compound V should not fire.

tests.name: edge-1: brainstorming mentioned but not completed
tests.prompt: Should I start brainstorming for this feature or skip straight to a plan?

expect.rationale: Brainstorming has NOT 'produced a spec' yet — the user is asking whether to begin. Compound V triggers AFTER brainstorming output, not at planning intent.

tests.name: edge-2: writing-plans mentioned but for a refactor that touches every file
tests.prompt: writing-plans gave me a plan for refactoring our payment module — it touches every file in src/payments/. Should I use compound-v to dispatch?

expect.rationale: Trigger fires (writing-plans output present), but Compound V's documented skip case ('pure refactors that touch every file — partitioning impossible') means the skill should LOAD then explicitly recommend the fall-back to default Superpowers. Loading is correct; the skill itself recommends the skip.

tests.name: edge-3: spec mentions only a CSS toggle (Sonnet-eligible)
tests.prompt: brainstorming output: 'Add a dark-mode toggle to the header — store preference in localStorage.' Should compound-v fire?

expect.rationale: Trigger fires. The skill itself will then determine that this is a 'pure UI tweak' per Phase 1A skip rule and recommend default Superpowers. Loading is correct; routing decision happens inside.

notes: Positive tests should result in the parent model invoking the compound-v skill.
Negative tests should NOT invoke compound-v — over-triggering is as bad as under-triggering.
Edge tests verify the skill loads but then correctly applies its skip rules.
No official eval harness exists in Claude Code (2026-05). Recommended runners:
- Manual: paste each prompt into a fresh CC session with the plugin loaded and verify the skill was/wasn't invoked
